description: Dry, viable seed may be required at a seed exchange; each stated feature is a 'kirema' (criterion). A criterion is a standard applied when deciding fit, classification, or eligibility. 'masue' (measure) compares something with a standard, while the criterion says how the resulting information will be judged.
articulatory_notes: Close the back of the tongue against the soft palate for 'k' and release into high-front 'i'. Make a brief alveolar tap or short trill before stressed middle-height front 'e'. Close the lips for 'm', keep the voice resonating through the nose, and open onto central 'a'.
usage_notes: A criterion may be necessary, sufficient, both, or neither; 'natheri' (entail) states the logical relation more fully than the label alone. The person, group, practice, or source that gives a criterion authority should remain visible when that authority matters.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Stating a criterion makes a decision's standard available for question and revision. A participant need not discover only after the decision that a hidden test had been applied.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: A useful criterion can come from skilled observation and tested fit as well as from numerical measurement. The word does not rank one kind of practical evidence above the other.

semantic_domains.cognition: a standard applied in classification, assessment, or choice
semantic_domains.wisdom: an explicit basis whose relevance and authority can be examined
